Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The task involves analyzing two POWL models of a Hire to Retire process for anomalies

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 1 is mostly solid but has a small flaw Interview is only reachable after Screen yet it has no forced order relative to Decide That means Interview could finish after the hiring decision is made which is backwards

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The rest of the flow Post Screen Decide Onboard Payroll Close is clean

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 2 is cleaner overall It forces Interview before Decide keeps the loop on onboarding and uses XOR for payroll so you can skip it if needed

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The only real issue is that Screen has no outgoing edge so it could finish after everything else which is odd

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Between the two Model 2 is closer to a normative Hire to Retire process because it preserves the logical sequence Interview Decide and the optional payroll step while Model 1 allows the dangerous possibility of deciding before interviewing

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The anomaly in Model 2 is minor Screen could technically run late whereas Model 1s anomaly is more severe because it breaks the interview decision order
